description:
Surfaced during mutagen-835 (SpecLedEx adoption). `.spec/state.json` is now a TRACKED generated-state file (SpecLedEx uses `git show <base>:.spec/state.json` as the append-only baseline, so it must be committed, not gitignored).

Problem: two mix tasks write it and they DIVERGE.
- `mix spec.index` — the canonical state-writer ("Builds index state and writes .spec/state.json"); compact and STABLE (re-running leaves no diff).
- `mix spec.validate` — also rewrites state.json, in a more verbose form (~1100-1200 extra lines of findings detail). Running validate on a tree whose committed state.json came from `index` leaves the working tree DIRTY.

Throughout mutagen-835 the committed form was standardized to `mix spec.index` output (each stage regenerated via spec.index before merge; final epic commit cdfad67 canonicalizes it).

Decision to make before wiring CI:
1. Which generator is the committed canonical — `spec.index` or `spec.validate`?
2. If CI runs `mix spec.validate` and then asserts a clean working tree, it WILL show drift against the index-form commit. Either have CI run `mix spec.index` (not validate) for the cleanliness check, or commit the validate form, or gitignore state.json and drop the append-only baseline feature.

See auto-memory note `spec-state-json-canonical-generator`. Possibly also worth an upstream question to spec_led_ex about whether index/validate should converge on state.json output.
title: Decide canonical .spec/state.json generator (mix spec.index vs spec.validate) before CI
